faq.question: What should I wear to a physique shoot?
faq.answer: For competition shots, bring your competition suit (or suits if you compete in multiple divisions). For more casual physique work, athletic wear, shorts, or whatever you'd typically train in works great. I'd recommend bringing 2–3 options so we have variety. If you're unsure, we'll discuss it during the consultation.

faq.question: How long does a typical session last?
faq.answer: Most physique sessions run 1–2 hours depending on the package. Competition day shoots tend to be faster since you're already prepped and peaked. Transformation shoots are typically shorter individual sessions (30–45 minutes) repeated over time.

faq.question: Do you shoot indoors, outdoors, or both?
faq.answer: Both. Studio sessions with professional lighting give us the most control for highlighting muscle definition, separation, and conditioning. Outdoor sessions can add a dynamic, gritty feel. We'll decide based on your goals and the look you're going for.

faq.question: Do you work with coaches or prep teams?
faq.answer: Absolutely. I regularly collaborate with prep coaches and teams to coordinate session timing around peak week and show dates. If your coach wants specific angles or progress documentation for their portfolio, I'm happy to work with them on deliverables.

faq.question: What's the turnaround time for edited images?
faq.answer: Standard turnaround is 7–10 business days. If you need a faster turnaround for social media posts or sponsor deadlines, rush delivery is available for an additional fee. I'll provide a few preview images within 48 hours of the shoot.

faq.question: What are the usage rights for my photos?
faq.answer: You receive full personal usage rights — social media, portfolio, personal website, competition submissions, coaching profiles, etc. Commercial usage (paid sponsorships, supplement company ads, paid licensing) is discussed on a case-by-case basis. I retain the right to use images in my portfolio unless otherwise agreed.
